sglang.title: Use custom allreduce w/ torch.compile
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
It makes torch.compile with tensor parallelism much faster.
llama 3.1 8b w/ tp = 8
python3 -m sglang.bench_offline_throughput --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --dataset-name random --num-prompt 1 --random-input 1024 --random-output 256 --random-range 1 --tp 8 --enable-torch-compile

sglang.title: [Performance]: Process affinity to CPU cores with multiple sockets support
sglang.author: HaiShaw
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Setting process affinity to designated cpu ids, to avoid unwanted process migrations (cost high in case of multiple sockets)
Modifications
Assign specific process to designated cores, handle multiple sockets, and HT (hyper threading: 1 Physical core runs as 2 logical cores)
Online benchm

sglang.title: Update XGrammar to the latest API
sglang.author: Ubospica
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
After XGrammar released their 0.10 update, there was a good bit of refactoring which seems to have broken the initial integration with SGLang. This PR works to resolve the issues and make XGrammar a working backend for SGLang.
Thanks to the great work by @gittb.
Co-authored-by: Ben Gitter

Checklist

 Add unit tests as outlined in the Contributor Guide.
 Update documentation as needed, including docstrings or example tutorials.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix chunked prefill with model dtype float32 on Turing Devices
vllm.author: wallashss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The issue is a bug on Triton with Turing devices, there are already opened issues there:
triton-lang/triton#3011
triton-lang/triton#3787
In a nutshell, it is a limitation of Turing devices to perform matmul with float32. The issue #9096 shows this behavior by casting the model to dtype=float32. A wa

vllm.title: [Model]: Add support for Aria model
vllm.author: xffxff
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add support for rhymes-ai/Aria, a multimodal MoE model.
Feel free to request changes!
You can try it with the following code:
from PIL import Image
from transformers import AutoTokenizer
from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams
import requests


model_id = "rhymes-ai/Aria"


def main():
    llm = LLM(

vllm.title: [Model] Enable optional prefix when loading embedding models
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Some embedding models use the checkpoint of *ForCausalLM, while others use *Model, yet their architecture names might not always match the expected weights. To improve flexibility, this PR enables loading embedding models (*EmbeddingModel in vLLM) using the weights of either checkpoint format.
FIX #

vllm.title: [Model] Add OLMo November 2024 model
vllm.author: 2015aroras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
An updated OLMo model will be released in November. The new model has a few small architecture changes compared to the original model:

RMSNorm is used instead of standard layer norm.
Norm is applied to attention queries and keys.
Norm is applied after attention/feedforward rather than before.

The

vllm.title: [bugfix] fix aria model and add torch.compile
vllm.author: youkaichao

vllm.title: [Feature] vLLM ARM Enablement for AARCH64 CPUs
vllm.author: sanketkaleoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Description
This PR enables support of vLLM for AARCH64 architecture. Motivated by the requirements from (#176, #5741, etc), I implemented this PR which enables the ARM path for CPU inference.
ARM Compatibility: Modified the build scripts and configuration files to ensure compatibility with ARM proc

vllm.title: [v1] EngineArgs for better config handling for v1
vllm.author: rickyyx
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This allows:

Users to use v0/v1 transparently with the simple flag change VLLM_USE_V1
When VLLM_USE_V1 sets, v1-specific defaults will be automatically used if users don't provide them.
This also allows easier migration in the future where we would simply replace the old EngineArgs with the V1's En

vllm.title: custom allreduce + torch.compile
vllm.author: SageMoore
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This Pr changes pynccl all reduce to be out of place and removes support for torch distributed's all reduce.

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove outdated init protocols
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR removes __init__ specification from the optional protocols, since every model should now accept vllm_config.
